purpose: Per-arrow seed populations for the Part B mechanism-level support route. Each arrow is a SEPARATE marginal meta-analysis drawn from its own mature literature; arrows do not need to share a plant/site/time (that is the joint-panel proof route only). See docs/PART_B_MECHANISM_META_STRATEGY_v1.md.
honesty_contract: candidate_syntheses are UNVERIFIED seed leads. A named review or meta-analysis is a starting point for a source audit, never an effect. No DOI, effect, or sign is asserted from this file; each seed must pass the per-arrow eligibility contract before it contributes to B1/B2/B3.

shared_eligibility_contract.trait_role_declared: the study measures the arrow's trait module (A_flower or B_flower) as an independent predictor, not a treatment contrast standing in for it
shared_eligibility_contract.outcome_denominator: the outcome carries a stated at-risk denominator or exposure (visits per unit effort; damaged per inspected; seeds per ovule)
shared_eligibility_contract.effect_scale_single: one declared effect scale per pooled envelope; scales are never mixed
shared_eligibility_contract.design_split: observational and manipulation effects are pooled separately, never merged
shared_eligibility_contract.uncertainty_present: an SE/CI is reported or reconstructable from declared inputs
shared_eligibility_contract.independence: clusters counted marginally within the arrow; multiple papers on one field panel are one cluster
shared_eligibility_contract.no_organ_substitution: leaf/stem defence or antagonism is not admitted to a floral arrow without a declared cross-organ bridge

arrows.estimand: effect of a floral attraction/display trait (size, colour, display number, nectar reward, guide) on pollination/visitation outcome
seed_queries: floral display size AND pollinator visitation meta-analysis
flower size OR flower number effect on visitation rate
pollinator-mediated phenotypic selection on floral traits meta-analysis

candidate_syntheses.descriptor: meta-analysis of phenotypic selection on floral display traits
review of floral advertisement and pollinator visitation rate

arrows.notes: Visitation is admissible only with an effort denominator; visitation-to-service proxy must be declared before it informs b_A.

arrows.estimand: effect of a floral attraction/display trait on floral antagonism (florivory, nectar/pollen robbing, pre-dispersal seed predation)
seed_queries: floral display AND florivory OR seed predation
pollinator and herbivore shared preference for floral traits

candidate_syntheses.descriptor: review of conflicting pollinator and florivore selection on floral traits
meta-analysis of florivory effects mediated by floral attractiveness

arrows.notes: This is the empirical crux arrow: its sign and magnitude relative to b_A*c_D decide the regime. Its B3 moderator slope is the headline conditionality test.

arrows.estimand: effect of a flower-specific barrier/defence trait (floral chemistry, trichomes, sticky/mechanical barrier) on floral antagonism
seed_queries: floral defence chemistry AND florivory reduction meta-analysis
floral trichomes OR floral secondary metabolites AND flower herbivory
flower-specific defence effect on floral antagonists

candidate_syntheses.descriptor: meta-analysis of plant chemical defence effects on herbivory (floral-organ subset)
review of floral (as distinct from leaf) defence against florivores

arrows.notes: The envelope must lie in [0,1] under the current model; any bounded mapping from a raw effect must be declared. Leaf-defence effects are excluded without a floral bridge.

arrows.estimand: effect of a flower-specific barrier/access-limitation trait on pollination/access outcome
seed_queries: floral morphology restriction AND pollinator access meta-analysis
corolla depth OR floral barrier effect on visitation
chemical defence trade-off with pollinator attraction

candidate_syntheses.descriptor: review of floral morphological filters on pollinator access
meta-analysis of defence-pollination conflict in flowers

arrows.notes: Must be a direct barrier-caused access/pollination reduction, not a generic trait-visitor association; a positive contrary sign is a context/trait-role finding, never recoded.

unresolved_parameters.attraction_defence_shared_cost: c_AD: bounded via the B4 break-even threshold (trait_architecture/part_b_support.py), never pooled from any arrow.
unresolved_parameters.assurance_outcross_dilution: c_R: no active Part B arrow; remains a declared sensitivity axis.
